card.intuition: A service that doesn't remember anything about previous requests. Every request must contain all the information needed for processing, allowing any server to handle any request.
card.analogy: A vending machine vs a bartender: a **Bartender** (Stateful) remembers you ordered 'the usual'. A **Vending Machine** (Stateless) doesn't care who you are; you put in a coin and a code, and you get a soda. You can go to any vending machine in the world and it works the same.
when_to_use: Enabling easy horizontal scaling (any server can handle the next click)
Building RESTful APIs that are easy to cache and load-balance
Simplifying deployments, as you can kill and restart servers without losing user data
gotchas: You still need state! You just move it OUT of the app server and INTO a shared 'State Store' like Redis or a Database.
Session management: instead of storing 'User Logged In' in Memory, you use JWTs (Tokens) that the client sends every time.

exercise.prompt: Where do stateless services typically store session data (e.g., shopping cart items)?
exercise.starter_code: storage = "An external __________ like Redis or PostgreSQL."
